title: Ariadne AutoCAD SDK Job v2
description: v2 job-request contract. AUTHORED ALONGSIDE v1 (schemas/cad_job.schema.json, ariadne.autocad_sdk_job.v1) which remains frozen at exactly 29 operations and unchanged. v2 is additive: it keeps the v1 semantics (operation + write_mode + args), and adds a structured target, an explicit policy block (write_mode/dry_run), idempotency, the originating agent, and an output_mode (summary|graph|ir). The v2 operation field is OPEN (no inline 29-enum) and is validated against the operation registry (config/operations.v2.json, schema schemas/operation_registry.v2.schema.json) at runtime rather than by a frozen enum, so adding catalogued ops does not require a schema edit. The 18 v1 per-operation arg if/then rules for the wired ops are carried over verbatim below for the operations that remain wired. additionalProperties true so richer callers may attach context.

operation.description: Canonical operation id. MUST resolve to an entry in the operation registry (config/operations.v2.json). The registry is the source of truth for which operations exist and their status; this field is intentionally NOT a closed enum in v2 (unlike v1) so newly catalogued ops are addressable without a schema change. A runtime validator rejects operations whose registry status is not runnable (catalogued/blocked/not_implemented => explicit error, never a fake PASS).

registry_ref.description: Path to the operation registry that defines/validates `operation`.

target.description: What the job acts on. dwg_path is the drawing; for write jobs it should be a staged copy unless policy.write_mode == write_original/live_edit explicitly authorizes acting on the active/original drawing.

dwg_path.description: Path to the target DWG. Read jobs may point at any readable drawing; write jobs SHOULD point at a staged copy under staging/ unless write_mode authorizes the original.

original_path.description: Path to the read-only original when dwg_path is a staged copy (provenance).

layout.description: Paper-space layout/tab name when space == paper.

write_mode.description: Carried over from v1 for backward compatibility. policy.write_mode (if present) takes precedence; when only this top-level field is supplied it is treated as the effective write mode.

save.description: v1-compatible flag: when true the active/original DWG is saved after a write job (router uses QSAVE, never Database.SaveAs).

policy.description: Execution policy. Supersedes the top-level write_mode/save when present.

write_mode.description: Effective write level. read = no persistence; write_copy = persist to a staged copy; write_original = persist to the original active drawing (QSAVE); live_edit = act on the live in-session document.

dry_run.description: When true, the job is validated and planned but no entity is created/modified and nothing is persisted. The result reports what WOULD happen (status ok with a dry_run marker).

require_staged_copy.description: When true (default for write_copy), the runner refuses to write unless target.dwg_path is a staged copy distinct from original_path (original-DWG safety).

save.description: Whether to persist after a write (QSAVE for write_original). Defaults true for write_original.

lock_document.description: Lock the document before write/live operations (matches the LockDocument-before-Run contract).

output_mode.description: Shape of the returned result payload. summary = compact operation result + diagnostics (default). graph = result plus a graph/lineage projection of affected handles. ir = result plus a full ariadne.dwg_graph_ir.v1 document of the (post-operation) drawing/scope. The chosen mode determines which result refs the v2 result carries (result_ref / ir_ref).

args.description: Operation-specific arguments. For the wired operations the same per-op required-arg rules as v1 apply (see allOf below). For catalogued/extended operations, args are validated against the operation's input_schema declared in the registry.

idempotency_key.description: Caller-supplied key that makes re-submission of the same logical job safe. The runner may dedupe/short-circuit on a repeated key. Recommended for write/live jobs.

source_agent.description: Identifier of the agent/runtime that issued this job (e.g. claude_code, codex, hermes, router). For provenance and audit; never carries secrets.

correlation_id.description: Optional id linking this job to a larger packet/convoy/run.

linetype.description: Existing/loadable linetype name; unresolvable names surface in the result's linetype_error, never silently dropped.

lineweight.description: AcDb::LineWeight raw value (hundredths of mm, e.g. 25 == 0.25mm; -1/-2/-3 == ByLayer/ByBlock/ByLwDefault).

point.description: 2D/3D point. Matches the v1 cad_job $defs.point semantics (requires x,y; z defaults 0).
